seo.title: Building AI Email Assistant with Semantic Kernel + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai email assistant with Semantic Kernel + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Email Assistant with Semantic Kernel enables intelligent email processing through plugins for intent detection, draft generation, and approval workflows. However, complex plugin chaining and planners introduce risks like cascading errors, invalid plans, and hallucinated memory recalls that can lead to sending inappropriate or confidential emails. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify outputs against PII, tone, and accuracy before execution.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Email Assistant Architecture with Semantic Kernel
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Email Assistant uses Semantic Kernel's plugin system for modular email handling. Incoming emails trigger an intent detection plugin, followed by chained plugins for draft generation, attachment processing, and send approval. A planner orchestrates execution based on memory of past interactions, while semantic memory stores conversation history.

Key components include: Email intake via IMAP/API, kernel services for LLM orchestration, native functions for email actions (send, draft, classify), and a handlebars planner for dynamic workflows. Outputs flow through verification before SMTP send.

This architecture excels at automation but risks data contamination across plugins and hallucinated recalls leading to compliance violations.

risks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
risks.description: Draft plugin fails due to bad intent classification, causing attachment handler to process wrong data
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies each plugin output before chaining with content accuracy and data type checks

risks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
risks.description: Planner selects send plugin without approval step for sensitive recipients
risks.mitigation: Vex workflow validation ensures required approval gates are included

risks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
risks.description: Agent references non-existent prior conversation, generating wrong response context
risks.mitigation: Vex memory verification cross-checks recalled embeddings against actual stored memories

risks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
risks.description: PII from one email leaks into draft for different recipient via shared memory context
risks.mitigation: Vex PII filtering isolates data flows between plugin executions

implementation.title: Intent Detection Plugin
implementation.description: Classifies email intent and extracts key parameters for workflow routing

implementation.title: Draft Generation Plugin
implementation.description: Generates email response based on intent and conversation history

implementation.title: Attachment Handler Plugin
implementation.description: Processes and validates email attachments before including in response

implementation.title: Send Approval Workflow
implementation.description: Final validation before email transmission with human-in-loop approval

vexIntegration.title: Vex Kernel Result Filter (Python)
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex verification to all kernel function results for PII and tone checking

vexIntegration.title: Vex Planner Validation (TypeScript)
vexIntegration.description: Validate planner-generated execution plans contain required approval steps

vexIntegration.title: Vex Memory Recall Verification (Python)
vexIntegration.description: Prevent hallucinated conversation history from contaminating email drafts
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
from semantic_kernel.memory import SemanticTextMemory

@vex.guardrail(name="memory_recall_verification")
def verify_memory_recall(recall_result: str, original_query: str) -> bool:
    return vex.run(recall_result, [
        vex.semantic_similarity(original_query, threshold=0.7),
        vex.fact_check("Conversation exists in email history"),
        vex.pii_filter(action='block')
    ])

# Wrap memory retrieval
async def safe_memory_search(kernel, query: str):
    memories = await kernel.memory.search("email_history", query, limit=3)
    verified_memories = []
    for memory in memories:
        if verify_memory_recall(memory.text, query):
            verified_memories.append(memory)
    return verified_memories
